channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Paula Abdul’s Teenage Years: Academic Success, Dance Training, and the Start of Her Career
item.description: The article looks back at the teenage years of Paula Abdul, highlighting the determination and talent that helped launch her future career in entertainment. Born in 1962 and raised in California’s San...

item.title: Sen. Cornyn relays concerns about Pentagon funding as Hegseth seeks support for $350B military spending plan
item.description: According to a report by Raw Story, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth recently raised concerns in meetings with Senate Republicans about the Pentagon’s available funding for weapons procurement, warning ...

item.title: Washington Residents Criticize Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Restoration After Algae Problems Emerge
item.description: Residents of Washington, D.C. expressed strong criticism of the recently completed $14 million restoration of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, a project promoted by President Donald Trump as part...

item.title: Tim Cook Warns Rising Memory Chip Costs Will Likely Increase iPhone 18 Prices
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook has publicly warned that upcoming Apple products, particularly the expected iPhone 18 lineup, may become more expensive due to a severe and ongoing increase in memory chip costs. Sp...

item.title: Union Leader Criticizes Emergency Response Procedures After Fourth Death at USPS Palmetto Distribution Center
item.description: A union leader is raising concerns about safety and emergency response procedures at the U.S. Postal Service's Palmetto Regional Distribution Center in Georgia following the death of a fourth employee...

item.title: Mining industry raises concerns over Mozambique’s mandatory state stake and mineral processing requirements
item.description: Mozambique’s mining sector is facing renewed debate following the introduction of amendments to the country’s mining legislation. The new law requires the Mozambican state to hold a minimum 15% stake ...

item.title: Columnist Argues Trump's Misreading of Foreign Nationalism Is Undermining His Second-Term Agenda
item.description: A Politico analysis by Alexander Burns, highlighted by Raw Story, argues that President Donald Trump's second administration is being weakened by a fundamental misunderstanding of nationalism outside ...

item.title: MSNBC Analyst John Heilemann Argues Trump's Recent Nicknames Reflect Cognitive Decline
item.description: A Raw Story article reports on comments made by MSNBC political analyst John Heilemann during an appearance on the program 'Deadline: White House.' During a discussion with host Nicolle Wallace, Heile...

item.title: Opinion Column Argues Trump's Support for Bill Pulte Reflects Concerns About Future Congressional Investigations
item.description: This opinion article by Jason Miciak examines President Donald Trump's decision to keep Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence (DNI) despite opposition from many Republican senators an...

item.title: A Retrospective of Frances Pellegrini’s Fashion Photography from the 1950s
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday highlights the work of American fashion photographer Frances Pellegrini, whose elegant and distinctive images helped define fashion photography during the 1940s and ...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Departure from South Africa Was Driven by Career Ambitions and Political Uncertainty, Says His Father
item.description: The article examines the reasons behind Elon Musk’s decision to leave South Africa in 1989, long before he became one of the world's wealthiest entrepreneurs. A commonly repeated claim has been that M...

item.title: Scheffler struggles in windy US Open first round as darkness halts play at Shinnecock Hills
item.description: Scottie Scheffler endured a difficult opening round in his pursuit of a career Grand Slam at the US Open, finishing with a two-over-par 72 after play on Thursday at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club in South...

item.title: A Collection of 40 Vintage Postcards Showcasing Jeanette MacDonald’s Hollywood Career in the 1930s and 1940s
item.description: This article presents a collection of 40 vintage postcards featuring Jeanette MacDonald, one of the most celebrated stars of Hollywood musicals during the 1930s and 1940s. MacDonald was an American so...

item.title: Don Lemon Suggests JD Vance May Be a Major Source of White House Leaks
item.description: A Raw Story report covers comments made by former CNN anchor Don Lemon during an interview with Legal AF host Michael Popok regarding alleged information leaks from within the Trump White House. The d...

item.title: CNN Panel Reacts to Report Claiming Trump Shared Document Comparing His Power to Historical Authoritarian Leaders
item.description: A CNN panel discussed a reported anecdote from the upcoming book 'Regime Change,' written by New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, which describes an encounter involving Presid...

item.title: How Automotive Advertising in the 1970s and 1980s Relied on Sex Appeal and Double Entendres
item.description: The article examines a widespread advertising trend in the automotive industry during the 1970s and 1980s, when companies frequently used attractive female models and suggestive imagery to market car ...

item.title: Legal Memo Questions Alaska's Decision to Remove Same-Name Senate Candidate From Ballot
item.description: A controversy has emerged in Alaska after the state's Division of Elections disqualified a Senate candidate named Dan Sullivan from appearing on the ballot against incumbent Republican U.S. Senator Da...

item.title: Colorado Attorney General Says Former DOJ Lawyers Are Leaving Federal Service and Joining State Government
item.description: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser, speaking on the Legal AF podcast, argued that the U.S. Department of Justice is experiencing a loss of experienced personnel as some lawyers choose to leave the ...

item.title: Obama Criticizes Trump’s Historical Narrative During Obama Presidential Center Opening in Chicago
item.description: Former President Barack Obama used the opening ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago to deliver remarks that included indirect but pointed criticism of President Donald Trump and recent...

item.title: SE Cupp Calls Trump Supergluing Gold Decorations in Oval Office 'Unfathomable'
item.description: According to an upcoming book titled 'Regime Change' by New York Times reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, President Donald Trump was observed in the Oval Office clutching a tube of superglue...
